topic: Selective-inference pivot for IDA/MPDAG possible causal effects. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are IDA and joint-IDA possible-effect multisets (Maathuis-Kalisch-Buhlmann), Perkovic/Guo-Perkovic minimal enumeration for MPDAG possible effects, and Lee-Sun-Sun-Taylor polyhedral selective inference after model selection.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive a polyhedral or otherwise explicit selective-inference pivot for the reported extremal IDA/MPDAG possible total effect after the same Gaussian sample selects an equivalence-class representation and an extremal parent-set regression branch.

source_refs.claim: IDA estimates a multiset of possible causal effects from an estimated Markov equivalence class when the total effect is not uniquely identified.
source_refs.author: Marloes H. Maathuis, Markus Kalisch, Peter Buhlmann

source_refs.claim: Maximal PDAGs/MPDAGs refine CPDAGs with background knowledge and adapt IDA and joint-IDA to possible-effect multisets.
source_refs.author: Emilija Perkovic, Markus Kalisch, Marloes H. Maathuis

source_refs.claim: Possible total effects in an MPDAG can be enumerated minimally as distinct functionals, removing duplicate reports caused by sampling variability.
source_refs.author: Richard Guo, Emilija Perkovic

source_refs.claim: Exact post-selection inference is available for Gaussian linear contrasts when the selection event is an affine polyhedron Ay <= b.
source_refs.author: Jason D. Lee, Dennis L. Sun, Yuekai Sun, Jonathan E. Taylor

source_refs.claim: Post-causal-discovery inference must correct double dipping, and selected graphs may define selected functionals different from the true causal effect.
source_refs.author: Ting-Hsuan Chang, Zijian Guo, Daniel Malinsky

source_refs.excerpt: native quadratic-regularizer selection range ... can be a genuine regularization artifact
Conjectures ... are not proved. The main open work is replacing Assumption...

open_problems.why_unsolved: The literature supplies IDA/MPDAG possible-effect enumeration and Lee-style exact selective inference as separate ingredients, while recent post-causal-discovery inference corrects graph-selection double use without giving a hand-derived event for the IDA extremal-effect selection map. The prior CausalSmith catalogue also has selection-range and extremal-sign analogues, but no direct IDA/MPDAG selective-pivot theorem.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to apply the Lee-Sun-Sun-Taylor polyhedral lemma directly fails because IDA/MPDAG reporting first passes through conditional-independence graph selection, orientation/minimal-enumeration logic, and an extremal possible-effect comparison, while Lee et al. require the selected contrast and event to be represented by explicit affine inequalities and Chang-Guo-Malinsky emphasize the selected-functional mismatch after graph selection.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A statement-level theorem giving explicit inequalities for the event that a named DAG completion or parent-set branch is the reported extremal possible effect, followed by an exact truncated-Gaussian pivot or a sharply stated asymptotic selective pivot for that selected causal functional.

open_problems.open_problem: Develop selective confidence regions for the entire deduplicated IDA/MPDAG possible-effect set and for the IDA lower-bound score min |theta| after data-driven equivalence-class learning and minimal enumeration.

source_refs.claim: IDA reports multisets of possible effects and uses summaries such as the minimum absolute possible effect as a lower-bound causal-importance score.
source_refs.author: Marloes H. Maathuis, Markus Kalisch, Peter Buhlmann

source_refs.claim: Minimal enumeration targets distinct possible-effect functionals because existing methods can report causally identical effects as numerically different under sampling noise.
source_refs.author: Richard Guo, Emilija Perkovic

source_refs.claim: Valid inference after causal discovery requires accounting for double dipping and can cover equivalence-class outputs such as CPDAGs via randomized graph selection.
source_refs.author: Paula Gradu, Tijana Zrnic, Yixin Wang, Michael I. Jordan
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Even for one selected coefficient, exact selective intervals depend on truncation limits induced by the selection geometry.
source_refs.author: Jason D. Lee, Dennis L. Sun, Yuekai Sun, Jonathan E. Taylor

open_problems.why_unsolved: IDA motivates set summaries such as min |theta|, and Guo-Perkovic resolves duplicate possible-effect functionals, but the scanned post-selection literature targets graph-based causal intervals or selected linear contrasts rather than selective coverage for a deduplicated possible-effect set or its nonsmooth minimum-absolute summary.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to union ordinary regression intervals over all enumerated parent sets fails because Guo-Perkovic show that sampling noise can split causally identical functionals into duplicate numerical reports, and the IDA min |theta| summary is a nonsmooth extremum with ties and near-zero branches.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A simultaneous selective confidence region for the deduplicated possible-effect set, together with a selective lower confidence bound for min |theta| that accounts for graph selection, branch enumeration, duplicate merging, ties, and multiplicity.

open_problems.open_problem: Give a selective pivot or simultaneous selective confidence procedure for joint-IDA vector effects under CPDAG/MPDAG uncertainty and linear-Gaussian SEMs.

source_refs.claim: Joint-IDA estimates effects of multiple simultaneous interventions under an unknown linear SEM with independent errors and studies asymptotic variance and consistency.
source_refs.author: Preetam Nandy, Marloes H. Maathuis, Thomas S. Richardson

source_refs.claim: MPDAG methodology adapts IDA and joint-IDA frameworks to estimate possible causal-effect multisets when background knowledge refines a CPDAG.
source_refs.author: Emilija Perkovic, Markus Kalisch, Marloes H. Maathuis

source_refs.claim: Recursive least squares consistently and efficiently estimates identified total effects under point or joint interventions when the causal structure is known up to an MPDAG.
source_refs.author: Richard Guo, Emilija Perkovic

source_refs.claim: The exact selective pivot is formulated for a Gaussian linear contrast eta^T y conditional on a polyhedral selection event.
source_refs.author: Jason D. Lee, Dennis L. Sun, Yuekai Sun, Jonathan E. Taylor

open_problems.why_unsolved: Joint-IDA and MPDAG recursive least-squares theory handle estimation and efficiency for vector interventions, but the scanned sources do not give a selective law for the vector of possible joint effects after the graph and jointly compatible parent-set collection are selected.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to run one Lee-style pivot per coordinate fails because Nandy-Maathuis-Richardson define joint effects through jointly valid parent-set collections, so coordinatewise conditioning ignores compatibility constraints among parent sets and dependence from shared covariance estimates.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A multivariate selective pivot or simultaneous selective confidence theorem for each reported joint-effect branch, with coverage statements that condition on the selected graph, compatible parent-set collection, and reported vector-effect multiset.

literature_map:
Maathuis-Kalisch-Buhlmann 2009 introduced IDA: estimate a multiset of possible total causal effects from an estimated CPDAG under linear/Gaussian, causally sufficient assumptions.
IDA reports set summaries, especially the minimum absolute possible effect, as a lower-bound causal-importance score when the effect is not uniquely identified.
Nandy-Maathuis-Richardson 2017 extends this to joint interventions, producing vectors of possible joint effects and asymptotic theory under sparse linear SEMs.
Perkovic-Kalisch-Maathuis 2017 moves from CPDAGs to maximal PDAGs/MPDAGs with background knowledge and adapts IDA/joint-IDA style methods.
Guo-Perkovic 2021 resolves minimal enumeration of distinct possible total-effect functionals and removes duplicate possible-effect reports caused by sampling variability.
Guo-Perkovic 2022 gives efficient recursive least squares for identified total effects under MPDAGs, including point and joint interventions.
Lee-Sun-Sun-Taylor 2016 supplies the core polyhedral selective-inference pivot: a Gaussian linear contrast conditional on Ay <= b has a truncated-Gaussian law.
Taylor-Tibshirani sequential-regression selective inference shows the same geometry-driven requirement for tractable selection events beyond the lasso.
Gradu-Zrnic-Wang-Jordan 2025 and Chang-Guo-Malinsky 2026 show post-causal-discovery inference needs correction for double dipping after graph selection.
Chang-Guo-Malinsky also stresses selected-functional mismatch: if the selected graph is wrong, inference may target a selected functional rather than the true causal effect.
The unresolved junction is exact or sharp selective inference for the IDA/MPDAG possible-effect object itself: graph discovery, orientation/minimal enumeration, extremal reporting, and regression estimation in one conditioning event.
The study-pipeline gaps supplied with this invocation concern LATE/Manski/interval-regression partial identification and were screened out as not matching the IDA/MPDAG selective-pivot anchor.
prior_proposal_map:
No direct prior proposal for eid_ida_selective_pivot, IDA, MPDAG, CPDAG, or possible-effects selective pivots was found under doc/formalization; the active qid currently has only state/pipeline files.
Closest inference/selection analogue: eid_proximal_phase_v1 studies regularizer-selected ATE artifacts and leaves the native selection range after nonunique bridges unproved.
Closest report-class analogue: eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier_v1 leaves localized report-class inference frontiers open rather than proving a usable post-selection pivot.
Closest extremal-effect analogue: pid_dose_response_iv_v1 leaves an adjacent-rank sign certificate and copula-radius phase transition unproved.
Other mined proposal gaps, including bounded-anticipation DID support intervals and k-source data-fusion hedge completeness, were not kept because they do not route Stage -1.2 toward the IDA/MPDAG selective-pivot target.
Overall, prior-proposal mining supports framing this run as exact-ID/selective-inference geometry for a selected possible-effect map, not as another orientation or adjustment enumeration problem.
